message:
fix(gateway): Correct idempotency middleware body re-reading for compressed requests

This commit fixes an issue where the idempotency middleware would create
malformed requests when processing compressed (gzip, deflate, br) or
chunked transfer-encoded request bodies.

Problem:
- The middleware reads the request body via c.req.text() to generate
  the idempotency fingerprint
- It then creates a new Request object to allow downstream handlers
  to read the body again
- However, the original Content-Encoding and Transfer-Encoding headers
  were being copied to the new request
- This caused downstream body parsing to fail because:
  - Content-Encoding: gzip indicates compressed content, but bodyText
    is already decompressed by the runtime
  - Transfer-Encoding: chunked indicates streaming, but bodyText is
    a complete string
  - Content-Length mismatch when original was for compressed bytes

Solution:
- Clone the original headers into a new Headers object
- Delete Content-Encoding header (body is already decoded text)
- Delete Transfer-Encoding header (body is fixed-length string)
- Calculate correct Content-Length using TextEncoder to get byte
  length of the decoded text (handles UTF-8 multi-byte chars correctly)
- Use the cleaned headers for the reconstructed request

This fix ensures that requests with any combination of compression
and chunked encoding work correctly with idempotency caching, which
is important for API clients using HTTP/2 or compression for large
payloads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
